userStories.title: Add batch role to PromptBuilder
userStories.description:
Add batch multi-story support to the existing PromptBuilder system.

Changes required:

1. `src/prompts/types.ts`: Add "batch" to the PromptRole union type.

2. `src/prompts/builder.ts`: Add a `.stories(stories: UserStory[])` method that stores an array of stories. In `build()`, when role is "batch", use `buildBatchStorySection()` (from step 3) instead of `buildStorySection()` for section (3). Skip the verdict section for batch role.

3. `src/prompts/sections/story.ts`: Add a new exported function `buildBatchStorySection(stories: UserStory[]): string` that renders multiple stories with the same USER-SUPPLIED DATA boundary tags as `buildStorySection`. Format each story as "## Story N: {id} - {title}" with description and numbered acceptance criteria.

4. `src/prompts/sections/role-task.ts`: Add a "batch" case to `buildRoleTaskSection`. The batch role template should instruct the agent to implement each story in order, write tests for each, commit each story separately with the story ID in the commit message, and use the injected test command. Do NOT say "test-after" — use TDD-aligned language (write tests, then implement, then verify). Include the `buildTestFrameworkHint()` output.

Existing tests in `test/unit/prompts/` must continue to pass. Add new tests covering the batch role in role-task, the batch story section rendering, and the builder with `.stories()` method.
acceptanceCriteria: "batch" is a valid PromptRole in types.ts
PromptBuilder has a .stories() method accepting UserStory[]
buildBatchStorySection renders multiple stories with boundary tags
Batch role template uses TDD-aligned language, not test-after
Batch role template includes test framework hint from testCommand
builder.build() uses buildBatchStorySection when role is batch
All existing prompt tests pass unchanged
New tests cover batch role-task, batch story section, and builder.stories()

userStories.title: Migrate prompt stage and remove dead code
userStories.description:
Migrate the pipeline prompt stage to use PromptBuilder for batch prompts, then remove the now-dead `src/execution/prompts.ts` file and its re-exports.

Changes required:

1. `src/pipeline/stages/prompt.ts`: Replace the `buildBatchPrompt()` import and call with PromptBuilder. The batch path should use:
```typescript
const builder = PromptBuilder.for("batch")
  .withLoader(ctx.workdir, ctx.config)
  .stories(ctx.stories)
  .context(ctx.contextMarkdown)
  .constitution(ctx.constitution?.content)
  .testCommand(ctx.config.quality?.commands?.test);
prompt = await builder.build();
```

2. Delete `src/execution/prompts.ts` entirely — both `buildSingleSessionPrompt` and `buildBatchPrompt` are dead code after this migration.

3. `src/execution/runner.ts`: Remove the re-export line `export { buildSingleSessionPrompt, buildBatchPrompt } from "./prompts";`

4. `src/execution/index.ts`: Remove the re-export line `export { buildSingleSessionPrompt, buildBatchPrompt } from "./prompts";`

5. Update any tests that import from `src/execution/prompts.ts` to either delete them (if testing dead code) or update imports.

Existing pipeline prompt stage tests must be updated to verify the batch path now uses PromptBuilder output (which includes constitution boundary tags, conventions section, isolation rules).
acceptanceCriteria: Pipeline prompt stage uses PromptBuilder.for("batch") for multi-story prompts
Re-exports removed from runner.ts and index.ts
No remaining imports of buildBatchPrompt or buildSingleSessionPrompt anywhere in src/
Pipeline prompt stage tests pass with updated assertions
Batch prompts now include constitution boundary tags, conventions section, and isolation rules
